name: Tri City National Bank
description_short: Tri City National Bank is a community bank serving Southeastern Wisconsin with 28 locations, offering checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Tri City National Bank has been operating for 60 years as a community-focused financial institution serving Southeastern Wisconsin. The bank operates 28 physical locations across Milwaukee and surrounding areas, employing approximately 400 staff members who are local residents. Unlike large national banks, Tri City positions itself as a relationship-based lender that prioritizes personalized service tailored to individual customer needs.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of retail and business banking products. For consumers, these include everyday checking and savings accounts, home equity lines of credit, auto loans, and credit monitoring services integrated into their digital banking platform. Business customers have access to dedicated commercial banking services with local market expertise. The bank also provides online and mobile banking available 24/7, merchant services through Clover and Commerce Control platforms, and credit card products.

Tri City differentiates itself through genuine local presence and community investment. The bank explicitly operates exclusively in Southeastern Wisconsin rather than nationally, maintains 400 local employees described as "your friends and neighbors," and actively invests in the community through competitive lending, nonprofit donations, and volunteer involvement. They emphasize security practices, with clear warnings against phishing and a dedicated fraud prevention hotline. Recent recognition includes a 2024 award for Best Mortgage Lending Company.

As a regional community bank, Tri City is best suited for customers who value relationship banking and local decision-making over the convenience features or rewards programs offered by larger national banks. The limited geographic footprint to Southeastern Wisconsin is a practical constraint for customers outside this region. While community banks typically offer competitive rates and personalized service, customers seeking extensive branch networks, premium rewards programs, or specialized fintech features may find larger institutions more suitable. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Credit monitoring with score and full credit report access
Merchant services through Commerce Control platform
pros: 28 physical locations exclusively in Southeastern Wisconsin with 400 local employees providing personalized service
24/7 online and mobile banking available for customers who prefer digital access
Competitive auto loan rates and home equity products with lower rates highlighted
Integrated credit score and credit report access directly in digital banking platform
Active community investment through nonprofit donations and volunteer involvement
2024 award recognition for Best Mortgage Lending Company
Referral program offering up to $500 for customer referrals
cons: Geographic limitation to Southeastern Wisconsin only—no national presence for relocating customers
Website does not display specific rates, APRs, or fee structures for comparison shopping
Limited information about credit card rewards programs or business lending terms on homepage
No mention of specialized products like personal loans, debt consolidation, or alternative credit products
Smaller institution may have less extensive investment and wealth management services compared to national banks
best_for: Southeastern Wisconsin residents seeking personalized, relationship-based banking with local decision-makers
Small business owners in Milwaukee and surrounding areas wanting local commercial lending expertise
Customers prioritizing community investment and local bank support over national branch networks
Home buyers in the region interested in mortgage services from a bank with local market knowledge
diagnosis: Tri City National Bank is best for Southeastern Wisconsin residents and businesses who value community-focused banking with local expertise and personalized service over the broad branch networks and digital-first approaches of national banks. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this bank exclusively serves Southeastern Wisconsin, making it unsuitable for customers outside this region or those who frequently relocate.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
